posts.author: BuyHighSellLow
posts.text: I think his question is more on the ability of the management to make accurate judgements than the effect of the write-off (which is nothing on the actual business itself)

posts.author: Pankaj_Motwani
posts.text: Already discussed in past, these write offs are notional and will not affect the net worth of the company

posts.author: atishay_jain1
posts.text: In FY’25 they did a write off and here in this video at around 10:30 minutes they said there won’t be any such write offs in the future. They again did a write off in Q4FY26! Can anyone explain/justify this? Its highly unlikely that they were not aware about possibility of write off when they said that.

posts.author: BuyHighSellLow
posts.text: I have been looking at Deep Industries Ltd. since some time now. I would like to share my two cents on its verticals and their prospects: Natural Gas Compression: In the short to medium term (say the next 1 to 2 years), I think this vertical should remain flat at worst and maybe a 5 to 7 %. So this will be the primary defence of the company to bring in sales and cash. As long as the field is producing commercially viable gas, the client will continue renting the compressors. Now, Deep has a 75%+ market share in the Indian charter-hired gas compression market and has a long history. Normally, D…
